page: Vultr vcg-l40s-64c-750g-192vram is a Cloud GPU family server designed for high-density parallel computing. It features 64 physical x86_64 cores powered by Intel processors with a shared CPU allocation and a single thread per core configuration. The system is equipped with 750.0 GB of system memory, providing 11.72 GB of RAM per core.

The primary accelerator hardware consists of four NVIDIA L40S GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined total of 192 GB of VRAM. For local storage, the server includes a 2600 GB NVMe SSD, which supports high-speed data access. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a high density of GPU accelerators and system memory relative to the CPU allocation, making it suitable for workloads that require massive parallel processing capabilities without needing dedicated CPU cores. The combination of 192 GB of total VRAM and 750.0 GB of system RAM makes this server well-suited for large language model inference, deep learning training, data science pipelines, and complex rendering tasks.
description: Vultr vcg-l40s-64c-750g-192vram is a high-density GPU server designed for parallel computing workloads. It features 64 physical Intel x86_64 cores with a shared CPU allocation and a single thread per core. The system is equipped with 750.0 GB of system memory, providing 11.72 GB of RAM per core, and a 2600 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. Acceleration is driven by four NVIDIA L40S GPUs from the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing a total of 192 GB of VRAM. This hardware profile is optimized for resource-intensive tasks such as large-scale machine learning inference, deep learning training, and data-heavy scientific simulations.
